examples.title: RPC Client Stub Example
examples.description: Example showing how client stub marshals parameters and makes RPC call
examples.explanation: The client stub acts as a proxy for the remote procedure. It marshals the parameters into a network-compatible format, sends the request via RPC Runtime, waits for the response, unmarshals it, and returns the result to the calling application.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - where n is parameter size

examples.title: RPC Server Stub Example
examples.description: Server stub receives request and dispatches to actual procedure
examples.explanation: The server stub receives incoming requests, unmarshals the parameters, dispatches to the appropriate server implementation, marshals the result, and sends it back to the client.

examples.title: RPC Interface Definition (IDL)
examples.description: Interface Definition Language example for RPC interface specification
examples.code:
/*
 * IDL (Interface Definition Language) for Calculator RPC
 * This file is used to generate client and server stubs
 */

// Define the RPC program
RPC_PROGRAM calculator:
 VERSION v1:
 
 // Procedure definition with parameters
 PROCEDURE add(a: INTEGER, b: INTEGER) -> INTEGER
 PROCEDURE subtract(a: INTEGER, b: INTEGER) -> INTEGER
 PROCEDURE multiply(a: INTEGER, b: INTEGER) -> INTEGER
 PROCEDURE divide(a: INTEGER, b: INTEGER) -> FLOAT
 
 // Data structures
 STRUCT OperationResult:
 value: INTEGER
 status: INTEGER
 message: STRING
 END STRUCT
 END VERSION
END RPC_PROGRAM
examples.explanation: IDL defines the interface between client and server. Tools read the IDL and automatically generate client stubs and server stubs in the appropriate programming languages, ensuring type safety and consistent data representation.

examples.title: RPC with Binding Process
examples.description: Showing how client discovers and binds to server dynamically
examples.code:
// Client Side - Dynamic Binding
PROCEDURE client_operation():
 // Step 1: Contact Directory Service to find server
 server_handle = RPC_LOOKUP("calculator_service", "v1")
 
 // Step 2: If multiple servers available, choose one
 IF LENGTH(server_handle) > 1 THEN
 selected_server = LOAD_BALANCE(server_handle)
 ELSE
 selected_server = server_handle[0]
 END IF
 
 // Step 3: Make RPC call using bound handle
 result = RPC_CALL(selected_server, "add", 10, 20)
 
 RETURN result

// Server Side - Registration
PROCEDURE server_registration():
 // Register service with directory
 RPC_REGISTER_SERVICE(
 service_name = "calculator_service",
 version = "v1",
 endpoint = "192.168.1.100:8080",
 authentication = "none"
 )
 
 PRINT "Calculator service registered successfully"
 RPC_LISTEN()
examples.explanation: Binding allows clients to dynamically discover servers through a directory service. This provides location transparency, supports load balancing, and enables servers to be added or removed without modifying client code.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for lookup after initial discovery
